title: O(N) heap allocs per frame in message render loop
description: main.rs lines 174-191: every widget call in the per-message loop passes &str or &String through egui 0.28 RichText::new(impl Into<String>), which calls .into() on &str, allocating a heap String. Affected per message per frame: ui.strong(&msg.sender_id), ui.label separator, ui.label(timestamp), ui.label(body), ui.small([edited]), RichText::new([deleted]). With N visible messages this is 4-6*N allocs/frame at 60 fps. Fix: pre-compute static label strings as WidgetText or LayoutJob once; pass owned data by reference.
close_reason: OPINION: egui's Into<WidgetText> trait bound requires String allocation per label per frame by design; pre-computing RichText objects moves the clone to load time but doesn't eliminate it; not worth the complexity at chat-scale (<100 messages)
